item.title: RoboBlitz
item.description:
Event Details

  • Organizer: EEE 2025-2029, IE(I) Electrical Chapter NSSCE.
  • Date: 14 March 2026

Event

RoboBlitz was a Robotics event organized by EE batch of NSSCE 25-29 in association with IE(I) Electrical Chapter NSSCE. The event’s main motive was to guide freshers to the world of Robotics. RoboBlitz consisted of three events:

    1. Debate Competition on Robotics and Automation and it’s effects on human creativity and employment aspects.
    1. A technical talk session by an Industry Expert, Mr. V Vijay who is also an alumni of NSSCE introduced freshers to basic robotics components, the underlying mechanisms and the pathway for them to follow.
    1. A technical workshop where the the participants were provided kits to assemble a Line Follower Robot. This is one of the most basic projects in Robotics.

Personally, I was was the Event coordinator. Through that experience I have been able to develop many valuable skills and got exposure to the processes that goes behind organizing an event. The notable skills I was able to acquire are:

item.title: International Physics Competition
item.description:
Competition Details

  • Name: International Physics Competition
  • Date: 16 February 2026

Highlights

This international Physics competition tested my physics skills as well as help me attain a new skill: LaTeX. I used LaTeX for preparing the solution document. This competition provided me exposure to LaTeX document writing. The question were really fun and interesting and helped me explore world of Physics in more depth.

Certificates of Participation

item.title: Hello World
item.description:
Hello!

This is my first post on this website!

item.title: Engineering Mathematics
item.description:
Certification Overview

Issued by: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur (via NPTEL) Instructor: Prof. Jitendra Kumar Course overview: Differential Calculus Partial Derivatives Matrices Differential Equations

Skills Acquired

As a First year EE student, this course on basics of Engineering Mathematics helped me sharpen my logical reasoning and problem solving skills. The detailed derivations and proof to each formulas helped me understand and appreciate the “why” behind every theorems.

item.title: Encrypta 3.0
item.description:
Event Details

  • Organizer: IEEE SB NSSCE - IEEE COMPUTER SOCIETY.
  • Date: 27 September 2025

Hackathon

  • The Event: Encrypta is a flagship event of IEEE SB NSSCE, in this event participants are provided a set of cryptic problems to which they have to find solutions via programming.
  • What I gained: I chose python as my programming language. The real world application nature of the problems provided me a valuable sneak peek into real world applications of programming. The entire process of building a solution from scratch and obtaining the output from the concepts I have learned from courses was extremely satisfying. This Hackathon motivated me to explore and study programming in more depth.
  • Beyond the Code: Second part of the event consisted of a talk session by Rakesh Ravi, Mentor IEEE SB NSSCE. Afterwards, a Group Discussion on the Effects of AI Integration in Tech was organized providing me an exposure to GDs and effective speaking.

Certificate of Participation

item.title: Drone Workshop
item.description:
Event Details

  • Organizer: SAE NSSCE AND FLYTUTOR RPTO
  • Date: 26 September 2025

Highlights

The workshop was conducted in two halves where the first half of the workshop consisted of going over the legal frameworks of drone operation in India and the theoretical concepts and electronics working behind the scenes. And in the second part we had the chance to build a small drone ourselves. This workshop gave me an insight into embedded systems in aviation.

item.title: Minimal Component Design Challenge
item.description:
Event Details

  • Organizer: IEEE PELS SBC NSSCE & IEEE IES SBC JECC.
  • Date: 24 August 2025

My Entry

As part of my submission, I designed a Motion Aided Traffic Signal (MATS) system that automatically turns on only when a vehicle is detected, thereby increasing power efficiency of traffic lights in remote areas. This event gave me a chance to experiment with minimal electronic circuits by using circuit building tools like Tinkercad.

item.title: Python for Everybody Specialization
item.description:
Certification Overview

Issued by: University of Michigan (via Coursera) Instructor: Dr. Charles Severance Courses included: Programming For Everybody(Getting Started with Python)Python Data StructuresUsing Python to Access Web DataUsing Databases with PythonCapstone: Retrieving, Processing, and Visualising Data with Python.

Technical Skills Acquired

This specialization covers the entire workflow of Python programming, moving beyond basic syntax into data application.
